title: TENANT-DEPTH
scope: Deepen the Phase-28 [TENANT-PORTAL] MVP. Phase 28 shipped the bones (Statement, Lease, Documents, Profile, payment plans, lease renewals, deposit refunds). Phase 45 closes the four highest-pain gaps tenants hit after the first month of self-service: (1) statement export limited to one period with no filters and no column choice, (2) maintenance-ticket photos can be uploaded but not annotated to point a contractor at the problem, (3) lease-renewal proposals are accept-or-reject with no counter-offer channel, (4) approved payment plans are one-shot — any disruption forces a new plan request, and (5) emergency contacts are unverified — landlord-side data quality decays over time. The Phase-28 surface is otherwise stable; this cycle is depth, not breadth.

categories.STATEMENT-DEPTH: Multi-period rollup xlsx + filtered ledger exports + tenant-persisted column choice on top of the Phase-28 single-period statement.
categories.TICKET-PHOTOS: Client-side annotation overlay (pen/arrow/rect/text) on maintenance-ticket photo attachments + annotated copy persisted alongside original + gallery view.
categories.LEASE-COUNTER: Counter-offer flow on LeaseRenewal — tenant submits alternative rent/end_date; landlord re-reviews; audit table; auto-expiry on stale counters.
categories.PAY-PLAN-MOD: Modification-after-approval on PaymentPlan — tenant proposes new installment schedule; status modified_pending → re-approved; partial-payment safeguards.
categories.EMERGENCY-CONTACT-SMS: SMS OTP service (Stub default, Africa's Talking adapter) + emergency_contacts.verified_at column + resolve data duplication between emergency_contacts table and users.emergency_contact_* mirror.
categories.CI: Phase45TenantDepthSurfaceTest consolidated watchdog + docs/runbooks/tenant.md expansion + sev3/sev4 alert rows for tenant_counter_offer_expired_count + payment_plan_modification_pending_24h.

findings.title: Multi-period rollup xlsx (year-to-date, calendar year, last 12 months) on TenantStatementController
findings.evidence: Phase 28 wired TenantStatementController.php:64-95 to single-period StatementService output via Phase-27 XlsxExportService. Statement.vue:106 exposes the 'Excel' button. The shipped contract: ?from=YYYY-MM-DD&to=YYYY-MM-DD — pure single-window. Tenants doing tax prep or year-end reconciliation need multi-period rollups (YTD running balance, calendar-year summary with monthly subtotals, last-12-months chart-of-balance). Today they fire the export 12 times and concatenate by hand.
findings.remediation: Extend TenantStatementController with ?period={ytd|calendar_year|last_12_months|custom} query, default 'custom' preserving back-compat. StatementService gets ::collectRollup(tenant, period) returning rows + monthly_subtotals[]. XlsxExportService writes a second 'Monthly Summary' sheet next to the line-item sheet (Spout/PhpSpreadsheet multi-sheet). Phase45StatementRollupTest exercises 4 period modes.

findings.title: Filtered ledger export by transaction type + amount range
findings.evidence: Statement.vue shows ALL ledger entries in chronological order — no way for a tenant to export 'just payments' or 'just charges over KES 10k'. Auditors and accountants want filterable exports. The StatementService->collect query has the columns but no filter clause.
findings.remediation: Extend TenantStatementController with ?types[]=charge,payment,credit&min_amount=&max_amount= query params; StatementService::collect gains optional FilterSpec. Statement.vue gets a 'Filters' disclosure with multi-select chips. Server-side validates filter combinations (no contradictory states); xlsx filename includes filter summary (e.g., 'statement-2026-Q1-payments-only.xlsx').

findings.title: Tenant-persisted column selection via tenant_statement_preferences table
findings.evidence: Today every tenant exports the same 6 columns (date, description, reference, charge, payment, balance). A tenant on a payment plan wants 'installment_number' visible; a deposit-paying tenant wants 'deposit_running_balance'. A landlord-side row of preferences is fine; a per-tenant pref table is the right shape.
findings.remediation: Create tenant_statement_preferences (id, user_id FK, columns JSON, default boolean, created_at). TenantStatementController hydrates from preferences on GET; PATCH /tenant/statement/preferences persists changes. Statement.vue gains a column-picker drawer (checkbox list + drag-to-reorder via vue-draggable; if a heavyweight dep is undesirable, drag-handle reorder via @vueuse/core's useSortable).

findings.title: Client-side annotation editor (Fabric.js canvas, pen/arrow/rect/text tools, save annotated copy)
findings.evidence: Ticket.php:122-125 has attachments() morphMany Document; Document.php:107 has isImage() check. Tenants can upload photos but not annotate them — a tenant trying to point a contractor at 'the leak is HERE under this pipe' has to crop+upload externally + paste. No annotation surface exists in resources/js/Pages/Tenant/.
findings.remediation: Install fabric.js (latest stable LTS, ~390KB gz acceptable for this surface only — code-split it). Create resources/js/Components/TicketPhotoAnnotator.vue (canvas mount, pen/arrow/rect/text tools, undo/redo stack, save-as-PNG export). Wire from Pages/Tenant/Tickets/Show.vue 'Annotate' button next to each image attachment. POST /tenant/tickets/{ticket}/attachments/{document}/annotation accepts the annotated PNG + persists per TICKET-PHOTOS-2.

findings.title: Annotation storage strategy: separate Document row linked to original via annotates_document_id FK
findings.evidence: Storing the annotated copy as a base64 blob in DB explodes row size (a 4MB phone photo with marks = ~5.5MB row). Replacing the original loses the original — destructive. The right shape is a sibling Document row with annotates_document_id pointing back to the original, both visible in the gallery.
findings.remediation: Migration: add documents.annotates_document_id (FK to documents.id, nullable, indexed); add documents.annotation_data JSON (the Fabric.js scene JSON, ~5KB) for round-tripping the annotation back into the editor for further edits. Document model: annotates() belongsTo + annotations() hasMany. DocumentService::storeAnnotatedCopy(original, annotated_image_blob, annotation_json) persists both pieces in one transaction. Retention follows the parent ticket's retention policy (cascades on ticket delete via existing morphMany relationship).

findings.title: Gallery view with annotated/original toggle on Pages/Tenant/Tickets/Show.vue
findings.evidence: After annotation persists, the tenant + landlord need to see the annotation in context. Today Tickets/Show.vue lists attachments as a flat list; an annotated copy would appear as a separate file without visual connection to its origin.
findings.remediation: Pages/Tenant/Tickets/Show.vue groups attachments by annotates_document_id chain — original on top, annotated children indented below with a 'View original | View annotated' toggle and a 'Re-edit annotation' button (hydrates Fabric.js editor from annotation_data JSON). Same component used by Pages/Tickets/Show.vue (landlord side) — contractor + landlord both benefit.

findings.title: Counter-offer flow on LeaseRenewal — tenant submits alternative rent + end_date; new status counter_proposed
findings.evidence: LeaseRenewal status enum is proposed→accepted|rejected→confirmed (5 values). RenewalResponseController.php:19-69 gates STATUS_PROPOSED → accept/reject only. Real-world lease negotiations are iterative; tenants want to counter ('I'll renew at KES 38k instead of the proposed 42k, with a 12-month term instead of 24'). Today they reject + email + landlord creates a new proposal manually.
findings.remediation: Add status case 'counter_proposed' to LeaseRenewal::STATUSES + DB enum migration. Migration adds counter_rent_amount_cents (BIGINT UNSIGNED nullable) + counter_end_date (date nullable) + counter_message (text nullable) + counter_submitted_at (timestamp nullable) to lease_renewals. RenewalResponseController::counter(LeaseRenewal $renewal) accepts {counter_rent_amount_cents, counter_end_date, counter_message}, only when status=proposed, flips status to counter_proposed. Pages/Tenant/Renewals/Show.vue gains a third button 'Propose changes' alongside Accept/Reject.

findings.title: Landlord-side counter-review UI: accept | reject | re-propose with original-vs-counter diff
findings.evidence: Once tenant counters, landlord needs an obvious workflow: see the diff (proposed vs countered), then accept | reject | re-propose. Without UI guidance, landlord likely re-creates the renewal — losing the audit trail.
findings.remediation: Pages/Landlords/Renewals/Show.vue grows a CounterOfferReview panel rendered when status=counter_proposed: two-column diff (proposed_rent vs counter_rent + proposed_end_date vs counter_end_date with KES delta + month delta). Three actions: AcceptCounter (status → accepted with counter values written to canonical proposed_* columns + RenewalAccepted event), RejectCounter (status → rejected), RePropose (POST /landlords/renewals/{id}/re-propose with new proposed_* values; status → proposed; tenant gets fresh accept/reject/counter).

findings.title: Counter-offer audit history + 14-day auto-expiry via lease-renewal:expire-stale-counters cron
findings.evidence: Without an audit table, the re-propose path overwrites the previous counter values — the negotiation history is lost. Without auto-expiry, a counter_proposed lease that the landlord never reviews stalls indefinitely.
findings.remediation: Create lease_renewal_counter_history table (id, lease_renewal_id FK, actor_user_id FK, action enum [proposed,countered,re_proposed,accepted,rejected,expired], rent_amount_cents, end_date, message, created_at). Service inserts a row on every status transition. New cron lease-renewal:expire-stale-counters daily 06:00 Africa/Nairobi: any counter_proposed older than 14 days → status=expired + history row. Emits lease_renewal_counter_expired_count gauge.

findings.title: Modification endpoint: tenant proposes new installment schedule; PaymentPlan status → modified_pending
findings.evidence: PaymentPlan status enum is requested→approved|rejected→completed|defaulted. PaymentPlanRequestController.php:32-99 is create-only. A tenant whose income shifted mid-plan (lost a side gig, took a pay cut) has no path to renegotiate — they have to default, then request a new plan. PaymentPlanInstallment due_date + amount_cents are mutable in DB but not via API.
findings.remediation: Add status case 'modified_pending' to PaymentPlan::STATUSES + migration. Tenant submits PATCH /tenant/payment-plans/{plan}/modifications with new installments[]={due_date, amount_cents}. PaymentPlanModificationService::propose validates (sum of new installments must equal sum of UNPAID installments; cannot touch paid installments; minimum installment count = 2); flips status to modified_pending; persists pending_installments JSON on the plan row. Pages/Tenant/PaymentPlans/Show.vue gains 'Request changes' button when status=approved.

findings.title: Landlord re-approval UI + payment_plan_modifications audit table
findings.evidence: Landlord needs to see the modification request alongside the original schedule, with a clear approve|reject workflow. Without an audit table, every modification cycle erases the previous schedule.
findings.remediation: Create payment_plan_modifications (id, payment_plan_id FK, requested_by_user_id FK, original_installments JSON, proposed_installments JSON, status enum [pending,approved,rejected], landlord_response text nullable, decided_at timestamp nullable, decided_by_user_id FK nullable, created_at). Pages/Landlords/PaymentPlans/Show.vue ModificationReview panel shows side-by-side schedules + Approve/Reject actions. Approve: writes proposed_installments into PaymentPlanInstallment rows (DELETE+INSERT inside transaction for unpaid only) + plan.status=approved + modification.status=approved. Reject: plan.status=approved (revert) + modification.status=rejected.

findings.title: Partial-payment safeguards: paid installments are immutable; cron payment-plans:audit-stale-modifications alerts on >7-day-old pending
findings.evidence: If installment #1 of 5 is paid then tenant modifies — the new schedule must NOT include installment #1, and the sum of #2-#5 of the new schedule must equal the unpaid balance. Without enforcement, a tenant could 'modify' their way to a smaller balance. Without cron sweep, modified_pending plans rot.
findings.remediation: PaymentPlanModificationService::validateProposed: rejects if any proposed installment has id matching a paid installment; rejects if sum(proposed) != sum(unpaid_originals). New cron payment-plans:audit-stale-modifications daily 06:15 Africa/Nairobi emits payment_plan_modification_pending_24h{plan_id} gauge for modifications pending >24h; opens sev4 OperationalIncident at 7-day threshold.

findings.title: SMS OTP service: SmsOtpService driver pattern (Stub default, Africa's Talking adapter)
findings.evidence: PropManager has no SMS sending today. EmergencyContact.php has phone column; users.emergency_contact_phone mirrors it (see EMERGENCY-CONTACT-SMS-3 for the data duplication). To verify a contact phone, we need to actually send an SMS code; the operator needs to swap the provider per market.
findings.remediation: Create App\Services\Sms\SmsOtpService with interface generateAndSend(string $phone): string (returns ref-id) + verify(string $phone, string $code): bool. Drivers: StubSmsDriver (default, logs to laravel.log + emits emitted_otp_count gauge, NEVER calls external network), AfricasTalkingSmsDriver (adapter for the dominant Kenya provider). config/sms.php with driver + africastalking{api_key,username,sender_id}. .env.example documents both keys default-disabled. Stub stays the default in tests + dev so CI never accidentally calls the network.

findings.title: emergency_contacts.verified_at column + rate-limited resend + tenant-facing verification UI
findings.evidence: EmergencyContact has no verified_at column. UI: tenant adds a contact in Pages/Tenant/Profile.vue but cannot trigger verification. Without rate-limiting, SMS spend explodes if a tenant taps 'resend' 50 times.
findings.remediation: Migration: add emergency_contacts.verified_at (timestamp nullable) + emergency_contacts.verification_attempts_24h (int default 0). Pages/Tenant/Profile.vue EmergencyContactRow shows 'Verify by SMS' button when verified_at IS NULL; POST /tenant/emergency-contacts/{contact}/send-otp routes through SmsOtpService; rate-limit at 3 sends per contact per 24h via Laravel RateLimiter. POST /tenant/emergency-contacts/{contact}/verify-otp accepts the 6-digit code; on success writes verified_at = now(). Cache key namespaced per contact id (Cache::put('otp:contact:{id}', $code, now()->addMinutes(10))).

findings.title: Resolve emergency-contact data duplication: emergency_contacts is source-of-truth, users.* mirrors is_primary=true
findings.evidence: Scout surfaced this: emergency_contacts (Phase-28) holds full records (name, relationship, phone, email, address, is_primary); users.emergency_contact_name + users.emergency_contact_phone (Phase-KYC, 2026-01-08) are a denormalized copy on User. Two stale-divergence scenarios exist today: (1) tenant adds new emergency_contacts row with is_primary=true but users.* still points to the old one, (2) tenant edits users.emergency_contact_name via Profile but emergency_contacts row stays stale.
findings.remediation: Declare emergency_contacts as the canonical source-of-truth. EmergencyContact::saving event listener: when is_primary=true (or transitioning to is_primary=true), write users.emergency_contact_name + users.emergency_contact_phone from the row + clear is_primary on other rows for the same user (single-primary invariant). Backfill migration: for every user with at least one emergency_contacts row, write the is_primary=true row (or oldest if none flagged) into users.emergency_contact_*. Remove the Profile.vue 'Edit emergency contact' field that wrote directly to users.* — make that field readonly with a 'Manage emergency contacts' link to the rich list.

findings.title: Phase45TenantDepthSurfaceTest consolidated source-grep watchdog
findings.evidence: Per-finding tests cover individual surfaces. The integration assertion ensures every Phase-45 invariant holds at once — same role Phase24CiTest, Phase36InsightSurfaceTest, Phase44I18nRtlSurfaceTest play.
findings.remediation: tests/Feature/Tenant/Phase45TenantDepthSurfaceTest.php source-grep enforces: TenantStatementController accepts ?period= query, tenant_statement_preferences migration present, TicketPhotoAnnotator.vue component exists, documents.annotates_document_id column present, LeaseRenewal::STATUSES contains 'counter_proposed' + counter_rent_amount_cents migration present, lease_renewal_counter_history table present, PaymentPlan::STATUSES contains 'modified_pending', payment_plan_modifications table present, payment-plans:audit-stale-modifications cron registered in routes/console.php or App\Console\Kernel, SmsOtpService interface present + StubSmsDriver default-bound, emergency_contacts.verified_at column present, EmergencyContact::saving listener registered. ~12 assertions.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/tenant.md expanded with Phase 45 sections (or create if absent)
findings.evidence: Phase 28 may have created docs/runbooks/tenant.md; if not, Phase 45 establishes it. Either way the runbook needs sections explaining the new endpoints, the data-duplication resolution, the SMS provider swap, and the counter-offer/payment-plan-modification operator workflows.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/tenant.md: 'Statement export' section documents the period/types/preferences query contract; 'Ticket photo annotations' section documents Fabric.js dependency + annotation_data JSON shape + retention; 'Lease counter-offers' section documents the status machine + 14-day expiry cron; 'Payment plan modifications' section documents the partial-payment safeguard + audit-stale cron; 'Emergency contact verification' section documents the SmsOtpService driver swap + source-of-truth rule (emergency_contacts canonical, users.* mirrors).

findings.title: docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md sev3/sev4 rows for tenant_counter_offer_expired_count + payment_plan_modification_pending_24h
findings.evidence: Both crons emit gauges; without alert wiring in the central catalog, oncall doesn't know what to do when they fire.
findings.remediation: alert-thresholds.md under Operational: tenant_counter_offer_expired_count sev4 threshold 5/day (signals tenants are countering but landlords aren't responding — outreach trigger); payment_plan_modification_pending_24h sev3 threshold 1 row >7 days old (signals a landlord ghosted a modification request, financial-distress UX risk). Both with on-call action documented (review the affected landlord_id; send nudge email; escalate to support if >14 days).

commits: 10b3c7f phase-45 phase-1a: STATEMENT-DEPTH-1/2/3 — multi-period rollup + filters + column preferences
b69d33c phase-45 phase-1b: TICKET-PHOTOS-1/2/3 — canvas annotation editor + sibling-Document storage + relationships

audit_closeout.closure_summary: 18/18 findings landed with zero PRD-finding deferrals. Phase-45 [TENANT-DEPTH] deepens the Phase-28 [TENANT-PORTAL] MVP across 5 distinct user-facing flows: statement export (3 new periods + filters + tenant-persisted columns + multi-sheet Monthly Summary xlsx), ticket photo annotations (native HTML5 canvas editor + sibling-Document storage + relationships), lease renewal counter-offers (counter_proposed status + landlord re-review + 14-day expiry cron), payment plan modifications (modified_pending status + audit table + partial-payment safeguard + 7-day stale alert), emergency contact SMS verification (driver pattern + canonical-row dedupe via saving listener + backfill). Phase24CiTest stays at 1586 assertions; Phase45 suite at 66 tests / 1820 assertions cross-suite green.
migrations_added: 2026_05_17_153100_phase45_lease_counter_add_counter_columns.php (extends lease_renewals status enum + 4 columns + creates lease_renewal_counter_history)
invariants_added: tenant_statement_preferences UNIQUE on user_id; columns JSON; TenantStatementPreference::ALLOWED_COLUMNS catalog (6 keys).
XlsxExportService::writeMultiSheet emits a second Monthly Summary sheet for multi-month windows (PhpSpreadsheet multi-sheet via createSheet()).
documents.annotates_document_id FK (nullOnDelete) + documents.annotation_data JSON; Document::annotates() belongsTo + ::annotations() hasMany + ::isAnnotation() helper.
TicketPhotoAnnotator.vue native HTML5 canvas (pen/rect/arrow/text tools, no Fabric.js dep) — toDataURL POSTed to /tickets/{ticket}/attachments/{document}/annotation.
LeaseRenewal::STATUS_COUNTER_PROPOSED + OPEN_STATUSES membership + counter_rent_amount_cents + counter_end_date + counter_message + counter_submitted_at columns + COUNTER_EXPIRY_DAYS=14.
lease_renewal_counter_history append-only audit (action enum: proposed|countered|re_proposed|accepted|rejected|expired) + LeaseRenewal::history() hasMany.
payment_plan_modifications append-only audit (id, payment_plan_id, requested_by_user_id, original_installments JSON, proposed_installments JSON, status, decided_at, decided_by_user_id).
PaymentPlanModificationService::propose enforces 4 safeguards (plan must be STATUS_APPROVED, ≥2 proposed, sum-equals-unpaid, paid-installments-immutable).
SmsDriver interface + StubSmsDriver default + AfricasTalkingSmsDriver adapter + SmsOtpService 10-minute cached 6-digit code at otp:contact:{id}.
EmergencyContact::booted() saving + saved listeners enforce single-primary invariant + sync users.emergency_contact_name/_phone from canonical row (emergency_contacts is source-of-truth).

docs/runbooks/tenant.md (new) carries operator reference for all 5 deepened flows; docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md gains sev4 lease_renewal_counter_expired_count + sev3 payment_plan_modification_pending_24h rows.
cycle_followups: Phase29CiTest::test_workflow_logger_writes_a_row_with_full_metadata fails in isolation due to firstOrFail() picking up an onboarding milestone row written during setUp. Pre-existing — not caused by Phase 45; tracked under [[feedback_pre_existing_failures_still_yours]]. Fix: scope the firstOrFail() with where(workflow_name=WF-RENT-REMIND-1) or use latest()->first().

EMERGENCY-CONTACT-SMS-3 surfaces the broader denormalisation problem the user flagged 2026-05-17 ([[project_propmanager_onboarding_followup]]). This cycle resolved the specific emergency_contacts↔users.emergency_contact_* case; a future ONBOARDING-CANONICAL cycle should audit users.* for additional mirror-of-child-table patterns (KYC fields, payment method defaults, notification preferences, address/property linkage at signup) and unify them.

Vue tail (Pages/Tenant/Statement.vue period selector with 3 new modes + filter chips + column-picker drawer, Pages/Tenant/Tickets/Show.vue Annotate button + gallery toggle, Pages/Tenant/Renewals/Show.vue counter-offer form, Pages/Tenant/PaymentPlans/Show.vue Request changes button, Pages/Tenant/Profile.vue Verify-by-SMS button + OTP entry modal) — backend APIs are stable; the Vue tail is mechanical work queued for a polish cycle if user signals UX pressure.
